subject: Geography
title: The Orinoco
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Orinoco usable.

hook.text: The Orinoco River flows through Venezuela in a vast arc, draining a quarter of the country.

buildup.text: Angel Falls, the world's tallest waterfall, feeds into the Orinoco basin.

discovery.text: A natural canal called the Casiquiare links the Orinoco to the Amazon—a rare feature.

twist.text: European explorers searched the Orinoco for El Dorado, a city of gold that never existed.

climax.text: Its floodplains create the Llanos, one of South America's richest wildlife habitats.

punchline.text: One river connects to the Amazon through a back door.

quiz.question: What connects the Orinoco to the Amazon?
